exemplar.title: How does the composition of the beers affect the half-life of beer foam?

detailedExemplarData.title: How does the composition of the beers affect the half-life of beer foam?

subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Logical progression from context through model derivation, data presentation, interpretation and conclusion
Clear structure with meaningful headings and sub-headings
Concise writing with most content directly relevant to the research question
weaknesses: Minor discontinuities in flow (abrupt figure/table inserts and unfinished sentence in conclusion)
Inconsistent citation formatting in Works Cited
Layout clutter from page-break artefacts and repeated captions
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration is coherent and logically developed (Band 3). The structure (Introduction â Body â Interpretation â Implications â Conclusion) is clear and accessible, though not âexceptionallyâ organised due to layout artefacts. The word-count is appropriate and material is mostly relevant, with only occasional repetition and irrelevant detail. These factors align strongly with a Band 3 rating, meriting 3/4.

strengths: Clear definitions of variables and parameters, with correct use of exponential and logarithmic notation most of the time
Effective use of multiple representations: raw data tables, semi-log tables, decay curves and linearised plots
Logical, step-by-step derivations accompanied by explanatory text
weaknesses: Occasional typographical slips (missing parentheses, inconsistent âlnâ capitalization)
Low-resolution graphics and incomplete axis annotations on some plots
Equations are not consistently numbered or referenced
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Mathematical communication is solidly at Band 3. The student uses appropriate notation and representations effectively, and the overall argument is easy to follow with good logical structure. Minor errors and inconsistency in figures prevent reaching Band 4. Therefore, a score of 3/4 is justified.

strengths: Investigation choice reflects genuine personal interest in functions and real-world application
Clear personal approach in selecting and contextualizing the research question
Moderate creativity in linking nutritional composition to foam decay half-life
weaknesses: Reliance on a single published dataset without original data collection
Standard SL-level analysis without novel modelling or advanced error-analysis techniques
Limited evidence of exceptional initiative beyond the core investigation design
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Personal engagement shows significant evidence of independent thinking, personal approach and creativity (Band 2). While the choice of topic is clearly tied to the studentâs interests, and the investigation path is personalised, it does not demonstrate the outstanding innovation or depth required for Band 3. Hence, 2/3 is appropriate.

strengths: Meaningful reflection on half-life values and acknowledgment of sample-size limitations
Thoughtful linking of results to protein/carbohydrate content and consumer implications
Clear evaluation of outcomes with suggestions for future exploration
weaknesses: Reflection lacks discussion of methodological biases (e.g., use of pre-existing data)
No quantitative error analysis or statistical uncertainty assessment
Limited exploration of broader mathematical or practical implications
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Reflection is meaningful throughout, with clear analysis of results and evaluation of outcomes (Band 2). The student goes beyond superficial comments but does not achieve the depth or critical insight required for Band 3. Thus, 2/3 reflects the strength of the reflection while acknowledging its limitations.

strengths: All derivations and numerical calculations (decay constants, half-lives) are correct and clearly presented
Comprehensive handling of raw data and regression analysis, including large datasets and multiple representations
Clear extraction of decay constants and half-life calculations with precise numerical values
weaknesses: No discussion of regression uncertainty (e.g., RÂ² values or margins of error)
Application is limited to a single SL topic without extension beyond syllabus
Opportunities for deeper statistical or methodological critique were not taken
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although some deeper statistical discussion is suggested, the studentâs mathematical work is flawless in derivation, accurate in calculation, and expertly communicated. The comprehensive data handling and correct application of SLâlevel techniques throughout merit the maximum 6/6 under Use of Mathematics (SL).

strandSelections.reasoning: Step-by-step derivations, numbered equations and explanatory sentences provide a logical narrative from premise to conclusion. Occasional typographical interruptions (e.g., âTo thenâ) reduce absolute clarity, yet the mathematical argument remains easy to follow, meriting a âgoodâ rating.

Derivations are correct and numerical calculations (decay constants, half-lives) are accurate to an appropriate degree. Minor explanatory slips do not affect correctness, so the student shows good understanding with mainly correct work, warranting band 4.

The student employs tables of raw data, semi-log tables, exponential graphs and linearised plots with regression lines. Axes are labelled and units given, demonstrating effective use of multiple representations. However, some graphics are low-resolution and trend-lines/axes scales are not always fully annotated, so the use is not âhighly effectiveâ.

The investigation follows a clear line: introduction of context â derivation of model â data presentation â linearisation â interpretation and conclusion. Each section naturally leads to the next and the student seldom digresses. Minor discontinuities (e.g., abrupt table/figure inserts and an unfinished sentence in the implications paragraph) prevent it from reaching the âexcellentâ descriptor, but overall coherence and logical development are solid.

Deriving the half-life formula, linearising data and connecting results to beer chemistry show moderate creativity and initiative. However, no original data collection, error analysis or advanced modelling techniques are attempted, so the criterion does not reach the highest band.

Results are linked sensibly to protein/carbohydrate content and consumer preferences, showing thoughtful analysis. Yet there is no quantitative error discussion or comparison with literature values, so the analysis is thoughtful but not âdeep and insightfulâ.

The student evaluates outcomes by ranking beers, discussing implications for producers and noting investigation limitations. The evaluation is clear but not comprehensive (e.g., lacks statistical uncertainty or concrete future modifications), corresponding to band 2.

The student reflects on what the half-life values mean, acknowledges limitations (small sample, limited beers) and mentions future exploration. This goes beyond a token reflection but lacks depth on methodological choices or broader mathematical insight, fitting the âmeaningful reflection throughoutâ level.

Variables, parameters and functions are defined, exponential notation is used correctly and logarithmic rules are expressed symbolically. Minor slips (e.g. missing parentheses, inconsistent capitalisation of âlnâ) occur but do not hinder understanding, corresponding to âmostly consistentâ rather than âconsistent and accurate throughoutâ.

The choice of topic linked to the studentâs âinterest in functionsâ plus the design of a clear investigation path reflects a personal approach. The work is more individual than generic but not highly innovative, aligning with the middle band descriptor.

At ~1800 words the report is within the limit and most content relates directly to modelling foam decay. A few repetitive statements about protein/carbohydrate effects and restating of formula steps add bulk, so it is âconcise with mostly relevant contentâ rather than âhighly concise with all content relevantâ.

Exponential decay, logarithmic linearisation and regression are all directly relevant to the problem and used appropriately. While effective, the mathematics is confined to a single SL-level topic, so it satisfies but does not exceed the descriptor for band 3.

Selecting an unusual everyday context, sourcing external data and deciding to relate half-life to nutritional composition shows noticeable personal thinking. Nevertheless, reliance on a single published dataset and fairly standard exponential analysis limits the evidence to âsignificantâ rather than âoutstandingâ independent thinking.

Headings, sub-headings and a flowing sequence (Introduction â Body â Interpretation â Implications â Conclusion) give the work a recognisable structure. Tables and graphs appear close to the text that references them. Some layout clutter (page-break artefacts, repeated figure captions) shows it is not âexceptionallyâ organised, but the reader can still follow the argument without difficulty.

All mathematics is solidly at SL level; the student handles logarithms, linear regression and half-life manipulation correctly, demonstrating full command of the syllabus material. There is no extension beyond SL level, thus the work is âfully commensurateâ but not âcomprehensiveâ.

annotations.comment: The title is concise and engaging, immediately conveying the focus on beer foam half-life investigations.
The research question is clearly stated and directly links beer composition to foam half-life, providing strong focus.
The introduction effectively contextualizes exponential decay beyond beer foam, demonstrating relevance to broader systems.
Consider acknowledging potential biases when using pre-existing data from a published study to strengthen reflection on methodology.
Numbering or referencing equations will improve navigability, especially when multiple equations appear in succession.
The derivation of the decay formula is presented clearly, with variables defined prior to manipulation.
When applying the natural logarithm, it would help to show the step-by-step expansion to $$-\lambda t$$ more explicitly.
The phrase âTo thenâ appears abrupt and disrupts flowâconsider revising for clarity and conciseness.
Table 1 header rows are misaligned and only display the first entryâensure full headers and consistent alignment.
Include a brief note on the provenance and reliability of the dataset to strengthen the evaluation of outcomes.
The complete dataset table (p.6) uses empty header cellsâlabel each column clearly for reader comprehension.
The large table of raw data demonstrates comprehensive data handling, essential for accurate regression analysis.
Consider adding a transitional sentence to preview the interpretation of regression results before presenting graphs.
Graph captions for decay curves are clear and descriptive, aiding the readerâs understanding of each beerâs profile.
The figure resolutions are low, and axes scales lack tick labelsâimprove clarity by enhancing resolution and annotations.
Adding regression line equations and RÂ² values directly on the ln-time plots would strengthen mathematical interpretation.
Table of natural logs is thorough but lacks bold headersâbold or shade header row to differentiate it from data.
The step-by-step derivation culminating in $$\ln H(t)= -\lambda t + \ln H_0$$ is clearly communicated.
Extraction of decay constants is clearly presented with $$m=-0.0038$$ and $$\lambda=0.0038$$ noted immediately.
The phrase âline of bestâ is incompleteârevise to âline of best fitâ for precision and professionalism.
Half-life calculation is clearly shown with substituted values, yielding $$t_{1/2}=182.41\,\mathrm{s}$$.
Specify units (seconds) for each half-life value to reinforce clarity and correct mathematical communication.
Linking the half-life differences to protein and carbohydrate content shows thoughtful analysis of results.
Quantitative comparison of half-life values (e.g., percent difference) would deepen the critical analysis.
The nutrition tableâs citation format variesâstandardize sources (e.g., APA) for consistency.
The implications paragraph contains a stray code ât85665ââremove or clarify to maintain professionalism.
The conclusion on p.14 is truncated; ensure a complete concluding statement to reinforce logical development.
Expand reflection on future extensions (e.g., error analysis, additional beers) to demonstrate deeper engagement.
Works Cited entries show inconsistent formattingâin particular, mix of dashes and italicsâapply a uniform citation style.
The first Works Cited entry lacks an author-date formatâensure consistency across all references.
Linking investigation choice to personal interest in functions shows genuine engagement with the topic.
Discuss the potential margin of error in regression slopes to deepen the mathematical evaluation of results.
The methodological overview anticipates key steps clearly, guiding the reader through the investigation process.
